Home»Lifestyle»Celeb News»Local Celebs»‘Champ!’: Chad Le Clos’s dad gets emotional after early Olympics exit

Despite crashing out of the Paris 2024 Olympics, Chad Le Clos’s father Bert is one of the proudest parents on the planet…

Despite Chad Le Clos’s early exit from the Paris 2024 Olympics due to a shoulder injury, his father, Bert, is beaming with pride over his “champion” son.

The swimming starfailed to qualify in the 100m breaststroke heats, his only event at the games.

However, he has vowed to return “in four years.”

In 2012, Chad made history when he beat US swimmer Michael Phelps in the London Olympics. He also participated in the 2016 and 2020 games in Rio and Brazil, respectively.

Despite Chad Le Clos’s evident disappointment on Friday, 2 August, his father, Bert – one of his biggest fans – revealed that he was beyond proud of the swimming star.

He posted on Facebook: “This is the champ at his best. Proud of you.”

In another, he posted: “What can I say about our boy? UNBELIEVABLE COMES TO MIND”.

This is the champ at his best 👌 👍 PROUD OF U CHAMP

Like Chad, Bert Le Clos has become a star in his own right over his endearing support and affection for his son.

In 2012’s London Olympics, Bert went viral after he excitedly cheered his son on from the stands.

“He’s my son. Look at him; he’s beautiful. I love him,” theemotional dad told the BBC. During the games, Chad won two silver medals in the 100m and 200m butterfly.

Just months earlier, Bert Le Clos had been diagnosed with prostate cancer. Sadly, Chad’s mother, Geraldine, also battled breast cancer and had to undergo a double mastectomy. They are both now in remission.

In an interview after his 2016 performance in Rio, Chad revealed how important his family support was.

He said: “It has been tough, to say the least, the last few months, but when you are swimming for something greater than yourself, it can help you achieve great things.

“I swim for not only my country, my coach, and my teammates but also for my family. During those last 50 meters, I think about them.”

Meanwhile, Chad Le Clos reveals that he remains cheerful despite his early exit from the Paris 2024 Olympics.

He told SuperSport: “I’m OK now. It is what it is.”

“I’m happy with the career I’ve had. It’s not done yet. I’m definitely in the departure lounge. Like I said, I’m a pensioner. The shoulder, the back, everything is falling apart. But it is what it is. We keep swinging. I’m not going to give up.

Chad revealed that he would be back for his fifth Olympics in Los Angeles in 2028 at the ripe age of 36.

title: Chad Le Clos's dad beams with pride despite Olympics exit
author: Megan van den Heever
description: Despite crashing out of the Paris 2024 Olympics, Chad Le Clos's father Bert is one of the proudest parents on the planet...
